Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings As To Respondents/Claimants Glenn E. Stamps And Allen J. Stamps, Individually And In Their Capacity As Executors Of The Estate Of Allen Stamps, Jr.
Off Calendar Mar-12-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Peter Lacques, esq appearing by Zoom for Junita Lewis (Petitioner). Kenneth Mitchell, esq appearing by Zoom for Junita Lewis (Petitioner). Steve Bournick, esq appearing by Zoom for Glenn & Allen Stamps .

Notice of Motion and Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings as to Respondents/Claimants is before the Court today.

Arguments held on Statue of Limitation on the filing, and Intent of the Will. At the conclusion of the argument the Tentative Ruling is adopted as follows:

The Court GRANTS Beneficiary and Moving Party Juanita Lewis's unopposed Request for Judicial Notice. The Court GRANTS Respondents/Claimants Glenn E. Stamps and Allen J. Stamps' unopposed Request for Judicial Notice. Notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in pleadings or transcripts. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.)
By the pending Petition for Determination that Petitioner Juanita Lewis Is the Person Entitled to Distribution of the Remaining Estate ("Section 11700 Petition"), Juanita Lewis seeks an order declaring her the sole beneficiary of the estate of her sister, Josephine Stamps. Such an order would entitle Lewis to what's left of the proceeds from the sale of 1216 Fulton St. Glenn and Allen Stamps, who are the children of Josephine's stepson Allen Stamps, Jr., respond that Lewis is not the only person entitled to distribution and, in fact, they're entitled to 50% of the proceeds from the sale of the residence. Lewis moves for Judgment on the Pleadings, seeking judgment against the Stamps' response and claim.
Lewis's motion is DENIED.
Preliminarily, the court rejects the Stamps' claim that Lewis's motion is improper. Where it is plain on the face of the pleadings that a Section 11700 claimant has no colorable claim as a beneficiary of the estate or a person otherwise entitled to distribution of a share of the estate surely the court can adjudicate that claim without holding an evidentiary hearing. (See Prob. C., section 11700.) The Stamps have not demonstrated that a motion for judgment on the pleadings is not a proper vehicle for such adjudication. Nor is the court convinced that the motion must be denied because Lewis failed to provide adequate notice. The motion focuses on the Stamps' response and claim and the Stamps have not demonstrated any prejudice.
The motion, however, fails on the merits. The Stamps contend that Lewis is not the only person entitled to distribution and that Josephine Stamps' 9/17/1993 will calls for distribution of 50% of the proceeds from the sale of 1216 Fulton Street to them (as Allen Stamps Jr.'s heirs). It is unclear whether the Stamps' theory is that the will is ambiguous but subject to their construction or the will is unambiguous but should be reformed to conform to Josephine's intent. Nonetheless, construing the pleadings liberally, Lewis has not shown that the allegations of, and the information in, the operative pleadings are insufficient to support the Stamps' contentions or the Stamps, as a matter of law, are not entitled to present evidence on their theories. (See, e.g., Estate of Duke (2015) 61 Cal.4th 871.) Additionally, on this record (and assuming the truth of all material allegations), Lewis has failed to demonstrate that the Stamps' claim to a share of Josephine Stamps' estate is untimely.
Mr. Bovarnick informed the Court that he sent a proposed order to the clerk in Department 204.

The Court admonished Ms. Peguese for arriving late again. Ms. Rasch stated that she received some limited files pertaining to the loan, but not as to the banking and insurance, and that she received no personal property. Ms. Peguese stated that all she has is the jewelry and a bed, that the bed belongs to her, that the jewelry is in storage at her Sister-in-laws (Valerie Burelson), that she does not have her address, but provided a phone number, that Ms. Burleson has a list of the jewelry, that there are no funds and that what ever funds were in the estate are going to be in Mr. Jones' accounting. Someone from Ms. Rasch's office will pick up the documents at Ms. Peguese's place of employment (address and phone number was provided in open Court) tomorrow at 4:00 p.m. The Court also ordered Ms. Peguese to call her Sister-in-law as soon as the hearing is concluded to advise her that the jewelry will be picked up and so that Ms. Rasch could communicate with Ms. Burelson as to the time and place for pick up. John Rueppel for Lisa Peguese (Former Administrator).
Ken Mitchell for Juanita Lewis (Petitioner).
Steven Bovarnick for Allen Stamps and Glen Stamps (Step-Grandchildren).

Mr. Rueppel stated that he was just retained yesterday, that he was not aware of the status report that was filed by his Client and that he can't speak as to the content of it. Mr. Rueppel also stated that he is here to lodge an oral objection as to recovery of assets that may be in the hands of a third party, that his Client does not have an objection as to the appointment of Ms. Dolch or filing an accounting, and requested 60-days to prepare a final accounting. Mr. Mitchell stated that if Ms. Dolch is appointed he is sure she will be pursuing a lot of the same things and that they are looking for monies from rents. Ms. Rasch stated that they are still learning all of the issues, that they will be looking for additional accountings with respect to collecting of rents prior to her administration and that she does not know where the personal property is. The Court removed Lisa Peguese as the Executor based on what she has done and what she has failed to do, and granted the petition for appointment of Debra Dolch as Successor Administrator (subject to review of the order). Bond is set at $30,000.00. The Court ordered Ms. Peguese to file an accounting from date of death until today and stated that the issue of wrong doing is moved over to Ms. Dolch.
